executiveSummary: Iteration 000029 implements standalone binary distribution for NVST via Bun's --compile flag. The implementation includes: a single-binary build script (src/scripts/build-binary.ts), a multi-platform build script (src/scripts/build-binaries.ts) producing darwin-x64, linux-x64, and windows-x64 executables, and an updated publish.yml GitHub Actions workflow with a dedicated release-binaries job triggered on release publication. Typecheck passes. All FRs and USs are assessed as complying.
minorObservations: The legacy release.yml workflow (workflow_dispatch only, builds npm package) remains in .github/workflows/ and may be redundant now that publish.yml handles full release duties. It could cause confusion but does not interfere.

The release-binaries job runs on ubuntu-latest and relies on Bun cross-compilation targets. Cross-compilation works well for linux and darwin via Bun, but windows binary cross-compilation from Linux may behave differently at runtime in some edge cases (e.g., path separators in embedded resources).

There is no automated step in the workflow to smoke-test the compiled binary (e.g., run ./dist/release/nvst-linux-x64 --version) before uploading. This means a broken binary could be published to a release.

US-004-AC01 requires verification on a clean machine without Bun/Node; this is satisfied by design (Bun --compile embeds the runtime), but no explicit CI step (e.g., minimal Docker container test) validates the binary is truly self-contained.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The implementation is fully compliant with all PRD requirements. Four minor issues are worth addressing: (1) consider removing or archiving the legacy release.yml to avoid confusion; (2) add a post-build smoke-test step in the release-binaries workflow (e.g., run nvst-linux-x64 --version after building); (3) optionally document in README how the self-contained binary is produced and what platforms are supported; (4) no structural refactor is required — changes are additive and clean.
